text: Herbert Simon was the first economist to frame the problem precisely. In 1971 he wrote that a wealth of information creates a poverty of attention. He observed that information consumes the attention of its recipients, so an abundance of information necessarily means a scarcity of whatever attention can absorb it. Decades before the web, Simon identified the structural paradox at the heart of the information age: every new source of knowledge also makes a competing claim on the finite resource of human concentration. The supply of messages has grown exponentially; the hours in a human day have not.
question: What paradox did Herbert Simon identify about the relationship between information and attention?

options.text: More information makes it easier to find what is relevant
The wealth of information is only a problem for people in rich countries
Information overload can be solved by better search algorithms
An abundance of information creates a scarcity of attention to absorb it
